title: Tool Discovery and Invocation
summary: Clients discover tools with tools/list and invoke a tool with tools/call. A tool definition includes a name, optional title, description, inputSchema as JSON Schema, optional outputSchema, annotations, and result content.
analogy: A tool catalog is like a weapons checklist: name, purpose, required inputs, expected output, and authorization before use.
diagram:
sequenceDiagram
participant LLM
participant Host
participant Client
participant Server
Client->>Server: tools list
Server-->>Client: tool schemas
Host->>LLM: available tools
LLM-->>Host: selected tool and arguments
Host->>Client: approved invocation
Client->>Server: tools call
Server-->>Client: tool result
Client-->>Host: result
Host-->>LLM: result for reasoning

drills.a: It lets clients, models, and servers agree on valid arguments before execution.

teachItBack: Tools are discovered before they are invoked. The server publishes names, descriptions, and JSON Schemas; the host bridges those schemas into the model's tool-calling interface. When the model chooses a tool, MCP carries the actual call and result.

glossary.definition: Request method for discovering available MCP tools.
Request method for invoking a named MCP tool with arguments.
JSON Schema describing allowed tool arguments.
Machine-readable JSON object returned by a tool result.

quiz.explanation: Resources are uniquely identified by URIs.
